reforms.description: In December 1985, existing employment and training programmes were amalgamated into two major schemes, the Job Opportunities Scheme (JOS) and the Training Assistance Programme, which was set to be replaced by the ACCES programme in April 1987, extending training options on a targeted basis.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby improving employability and productivity, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The ACCES programme is due to replace the Training Assistance Programme in April 1987.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant restructuring of employment and training initiatives, focusing on targeted training to improve job outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: In December 1985 existing employment and training programmes were amalgamated into two major schemes managed by the Department of Labour - the Job Opportunities Scheme (JOS) and the Training Assistance Programme; the latter is due to be replaced in April 1987 by the ACCES programme which extends the range of training options on a targeted basis.
